[*Time inconsistency*]{.underline}: apply **hyperbolic discounting** to model the prioritization of immediate rewards over long-term ones.

<div style="margin-top: -0.5em; margin-bottom: -0.5em;">
\begin{aligned}
&P(a' \mid s, {\color{red}d}) \propto \exp(\alpha Q(s, a' \mid {\color{red}d})) \\
&Q(s, a \mid {\color{red}d}) = {\color{red}\frac{1}{1 + kd}}R(s,a) + \underset{s',~a'}{\mathbb{E}}[Q(s', a' \mid {\color{red}d + 1})]
\end{aligned}
</div>


<div style="display: flex; gap: 1em;">
<div style="flex: 1; margin-top: -2em;">

![](images/discounting.svg){width="80%"}

</div>
<div style="flex: 1;">

Naive: $a' \sim P(\cdot \mid s, d)$

Sophisticated: $a' \sim P(\cdot \mid s, 0)$

[@inconsistent]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}.
</div>
</div>
